source: Live sim refresh (Regime B + intra-row pipelining + softmax row-parallel, Layout A) on 2026-04-25
note: Regenerate via gen_expected_cycles.py after any sim-model change. AH configs (attn_head) are manually maintained — refresh after running test.py --model attention. The 2026-04-25 entries reflect the softmax row-parallel fix (azurelily/IMC/scheduler_stats/scheduler.py:_run_softmax_exp/_run_softmax_norm now multiply per-row latency by rows_per_lane=ceil(rows/W_softmax) so the outer rows axis is W-parallel, in addition to the pre-existing within-row /W split). 2026-04-25 ADVISORY MARKING: For AH configs (attn_head), this file is now ADVISORY ONLY — the canonical Stage 1 gate uses Fmax-independent counters (see expected_counters.json + run_checks.py --gate counters). Cycle values here are derived from a placeholder Fmax (no VTR data yet); they remain useful as diagnostic but are no longer load-bearing. DIMM-top + FC Phase-2 configs continue to gate on cycles (those have real Fmax from prior VTR runs).

azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.todo: Stage names map to Azure-Lily modules: score=mac_qk, softmax=clb_softmax (S_LOAD+S_INV+S_NORM), wsum=mac_sv. Residuals annotated in phase5_known_deltas.json.

nldpe_attn_head_d64_c128.note: Captured from azurelily/IMC/test.py --model attention --seq_length 128 --head_dim 64 --imc_file configs/nl_dpe.json post-2026-04-27 per-Q-row cascade model in azurelily/IMC/scheduler_stats/scheduler.py::run_attention_per_Q_row_cascade. The new model replaces the idealised 'stages run sequentially with intra-stage row pipelining' assumption with a strict per-Q-row cascade matching the RTL head FSM (Architecture B / commit 8f7b1bb): K/V loaded ONCE in S_LOAD_KV, then N=128 Q rows iterated through mac_qk → softmax → mac_sv per row before advancing. Per-stage cycles now reflect the OR-aggregated active span across all W=16 DIMM lanes (≈ entire DIMM cascade window), aligned with the RTL TB's AH_*_STAGES_TOTAL probe semantics. Pre-2026-04-27 (idealised pipeline): linear_qkv=2424, mac_qk=5428, softmax_exp=41, softmax_norm=24, mac_sv=5077, e2e=12993. Post-2026-04-27 cascade: stages now ≈ 73K cyc each (full cascade window) and e2e ≈ 78K cyc, within ±5% of the RTL TB measurements. Cascade cycles produced via ns = arch_cycles × t_clk_at_target_Fmax (= 9.7182 ns) so the JSON consumer recovers arch_cycles via ns / t_clk_at_Fmax exactly. linear_qkv keeps the existing _run_linear convention (sim_ns / t_clk_at_Fmax) so its delta stays at +891 as documented in phase7_known_deltas.json.

azurelily_attn_head_d64_c128.note: Captured from azurelily/IMC/test.py --model attention --seq_length 128 --head_dim 64 --imc_file configs/azure_lily.json POST AH B-5 finish (2026-04-27 K/V amortization). AL head FSM rewritten to mirror NL's K/V amortization: K/V loaded ONCE in S_LOAD_KV (3341 cyc), then 128 Q rows iterate through DIMM cascade (each ~2450 cyc = mac_qk S_FEED_QK 2304 + softmax S_NORM tail ~130 + glue 16). Pre-B-5 finish (legacy soft-rst): e2e=517788. Post-B-5 finish (K/V amortization): e2e=324205 (1.6× speedup from K/V load amortization). Sim cycles match RTL within ±2% per stage / ±1% E2E (RTL=321661, sim=324205, Δ=-2544). The AL DIMM is bottlenecked by mac_qk's redundant-lane topology (W=16 lanes all process the SAME K row, no parallelism along K) — a deeper architectural change is needed to close the perf gap with NL (NL E2E=79033 cyc; AL E2E=321661 cyc; the 4× delta is from AL's redundant lanes vs NL's true W=16 lane parallelism).
